design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate castle sections, collect power-ups (fireball, shield), defeat obstacles and enemies, reach and topple the Black Knight
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (limited rocks) balanced against enemy variety and environmental hazards; disorientation mechanic creates vulnerability windows
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Young hero Duncan infiltrating an evil castle to overthrow the tyrannical Black Knight; progressing through themed domains to reach the final confrontation
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Innovative control scheme (WASD + mouse for aiming while keyboard for movement); disorientation as strategic vulnerability; dungeon trap (Trouble 3) as resource farm rather than pure punishment; multi-path progression through randomized doors

difficulty.checkpointing: Falling into floor holes leads to Trouble 3 dungeon, which functions as delay penalty on easier difficulties but becomes strategically exploitable on harder difficulties for resource gathering
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Three selectable skill levels (normal, advanced); Advanced features more enemies and additional surprises; player can strategically use dungeon trap to replenish rock supply on harder difficulties

progression.upgrade_system: Collectible power-ups: Magic Shield (defensive) and Fireball ability (offensive); limited rock ammunition replenished through scattered bags and dungeon farming
progression.unlock_mechanism: Four doors in Great Hall lead to themed level sequences; Shield and Fireball doors are fixed; two doors alternate between Trouble and Fireball courses (randomized on repeated visits)
replayability_hooks: Three difficulty levels with different enemy placement and surprises
Random door assignments create variable progression paths
Strategic resource management (rock conservation vs. spending)
Dungeon trap exploitation for speedrunning or resource optimization

world_building_techniques: Visual castle architecture defining four thematic sections (Fireball, Shield, Trouble, Black Knight)
Atmospheric opening: storm clouds backdrop and Bach's Toccata and Fugue in D minor
Enemy variety (rats, bats) suggests castle ecosystem
Final cutscene: toppling throne and gargoyle rescue concludes confrontation

boss_design.philosophy: Single antagonist (Black Knight) serves as final goal state rather than traditional boss encounter; victory achieved by pulling levers to topple his throne, then witnessing his defeat animation
notable_bosses: Black Knight - Final adversary; defeated by collecting Shield and Fireball, pulling levers in throne room

faction_indicators: Townspeople terrorized by Black Knight (implied peasantry under oppression)
Black Knight as clear antagonist (tyrannical ruler)
danger_signals: Castle sections named 'Trouble' indicating hazardous zones
Rat and bat infestations suggest decay and danger
Disorientation mechanic creates vulnerability - loss of agency
environmental_storytelling: Castle geography divided into functional domains (Fireball chamber, Shield chamber, Trouble dungeons, Throne room)
Scattered resources (rock bags, elixirs) suggest previous inhabitants or preparation
Climbable ropes and walkable ledges depict medieval castle architecture
Storm clouds and Gothic aesthetic establish hostile atmosphere
